human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for fintech-specific source quality, sponsor-bank and processor dependency handling, access and change authority boundaries, evidence traceability, and validated delivery completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to fintech and embedded-finance platform support work grounded in sponsor-bank, processor, ledger, API, IAM, change, and observability records.

Authority sources cover interagency third-party risk guidance, bank-fintech due diligence guidance, bank service provider incident notification rules, FFIEC operations and payment workflow references, and PCI DSS control evidence expectations.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope fintech execution, missing evidence, policy-exception refusal, security handoff, cross-team dependency routing, change-readiness blockers, source conflict, overgeneralized fintech requests, repeat failure escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around access approval, release approval, security forensics, fraud and compliance judgments, and unsupported workaround requests.

notes: The package is suitable as a validated fintech and embedded-finance financial-platform-support specialist for spec-pack distribution and JIT materialization under orchestrator control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
